books.title: Phineas Finn
books.author: Anthony Trollope
books.summary: A young Irishman attempts to establish himself as a member of Parliament while finding his way in high society.

books.title: The Last of the Mohicans
books.author: James Fenimore Cooper
books.summary: Frontiersmen and Native Americans help transport a colonel’s daughters through upper New York during the French and Indian War.

books.title: The Autobiography of Calvin Coolidge
books.author: Calvin Coolidge
books.summary: The thirtieth president of the United States recounts his life story.

books.title: The Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers
books.author: Diogenes Laërtius
books.summary: Brief biographies of the prominent philosophers of ancient Greece.

books.title: Winnie-the-Pooh
books.author: A. A. Milne
books.summary: A father tells stories to his son about the son’s stuffed animals come to life.

books.title: Back to Methuselah
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: Five linked plays explore human longevity and governance.

books.title: As You Like It
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: Forced to flee to the Forest of Arden, the daughter of a duke encounters a variety of characters, her banished father, and even finds love.

books.title: Through the Looking-Glass
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.summary: In a follow-up to her adventures in Wonderland, Alice finds herself in a mirror image of the whimsical land where she becomes a queen in her own right.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of P. G. Wodehouse’s short fiction.

books.title: The Portent
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A young Scottish tutor, gifted with a supernatural sense, discovers a haunting connection to a somnambulant noblewoman while residing at a remote English estate.

books.title: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
books.author: Mahatma Gandhi
books.summary: Gandhi relates his life experiences from his birth in Gujarat in 1869 through the Indian National Congress of 1915.

books.title: Kate Plus 10
books.author: Edgar Wallace
books.summary: A police inspector investigates an enigmatic young woman and her connection with various criminal activities.

books.title: A Week on the Concord and Merrimack Rivers
books.author: Henry David Thoreau
books.summary: On a rowboat excursion with his brother, Thoreau lets his thoughts meander with the river.

books.title: The Theory of Moral Sentiments
books.author: Adam Smith
books.summary: Adam Smith assesses the sources of human morality and the role of human sympathies in judging the propriety of our actions and behaviors.

books.title: Mirèio
books.author: Frédéric Mistral
books.summary: Two young Provençal lovers are kept apart by their social class.

books.title: Black No More
books.author: George Schuyler
books.summary: A medical process that makes black people look white revolutionizes American society.

books.title: The Last Man
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.summary: Love and war in future Europe are thrown into confusion by a global pandemic that obliterates humanity.

books.title: Irish Fairy Tales
books.author: James Stephens
books.summary: Ten stories from the Fenian cycle of Irish folklore retold by James Stephens.

books.title: The Four Men
books.author: Hilaire Belloc
books.summary: Hilaire Belloc takes a pilgrimage across his beloved home English county of Sussex writing as four distinct personalities, with a wide variety of songs, stories, and digressions.

books.title: On a Chinese Screen
books.author: W. Somerset Maugham
books.summary: A collection of short vignettes about the people and places W. Somerset Maugham encountered during his travels along the Yangtze River in 1919–20.
